headlines.title: How the White House Correspondents' Association dinner chaos unfolded
headlines.description: Hundreds of journalists, celebrities and politicians, including President Donald Trump and the two men next in line to succeed him, had taken their seats at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner Saturday night when, one floor above, a man rushed toward a Washington Hilton Hotel security checkpoint armed with guns and knives

headlines.title: King Charles to reaffirm U.K.-U.S. relationship in rare address to Congress
headlines.description: King Charles III plans to reaffirm the United Kingdom and the United States’ long “special relationship” and common values of democracy and freedom in a rare speech to Congress on Tuesday.

headlines.title: Group says it won't drop its White House ballroom lawsuit, despite DOJ pressure
headlines.description: The National Trust for Historic Preservation said it will not drop its lawsuit to halt construction of Trump's White House ballroom, despite the Justice Department's request.

headlines.title: Trump welcomes King Charles: 'Wounds of war healed into the most cherished friendship'
headlines.description: President Trump spoke at a White House ceremony for King Charles and Queen Camilla. The president praised the "noble spirit of the British" and recalled the historic partnership between the nations.

headlines.title: Trump greets King Charles and Queen Camilla at the White House
headlines.description: President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump greeted King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the White House as part of their state visit. Charles is expected to later address a joint meeting of Congress.

headlines.title: Jimmy Kimmel Responds After Trumps Call for ABC to Fire Him
headlines.description: Jimmy Kimmel is responding to calls from President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump for him to be fired by ABC after a morbid joke he made during a parody of the White House Correspondents’ Dinner. The joke aired two days before prosecutors say a gunman tried to assassinate the president at the event. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Trump may send Afghan allies who were promised new lives in the U.S. to Congo instead
headlines.description: Hundreds of Afghan refugees who helped the United States fight the Taliban may be sent to the Democratic Republic of Congo after the Trump administration halted their U.S. resettlement, an advocacy group said

headlines.title: A year after DOGE, former federal employees are still looking for work
headlines.description: After he applied to his 599th job, John Burg stopped counting

headlines.title: How the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner chaos unfolded
headlines.description: Secret Service faced challenges evacuating President, Vice President, Cabinet officials and Congressional leaders after gunman rushes D.C. gala.

headlines.title: U.S. appears cool on Iran proposal to end war and reopen Hormuz without a nuclear deal
headlines.description: The United States showed little immediate enthusiasm for a new Iranian proposal that would end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz without resolving the impasse over the Islamic Republic's nuclear program.

headlines.title: Trump officials point finger at Democrats and the media for political violence
headlines.description: The president struck a conciliatory tone Saturday night, but administration officials later accused journalists and Democrats of fueling divisive rhetoric.

headlines.title: Iran presented Trump with proposal to open Strait of Hormuz, but delay nuclear talks
headlines.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi flew to meet Russia's Vladimir Putin after Donald Trump withdrew his envoys from peace talks in Pakistan.
